resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def findComplement(self, num: int) -> int:
        return num ^ ((1 << num.bit_length()) - 1)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses bitwise operations to find the complement of the given integer. The expression `(1 << num.bit_length()) - 1` generates a binary number with all bits set to 1, and then the XOR operation (`^`) is used to flip all the bits of the input number, resulting in its complement.
